sanskrit: युक्तः कर्मफलं त्यक्त्वा शान्तिमाप्नोति नैष्ठिकीम् । अयुक्तः कामकारेण फले सक्तो निबध्यते ॥
hindiTranslation: कर्मयोगी कर्मफलका त्याग करके नैष्ठिकी शान्तिको प्राप्त होता है ।  परन्तु सकाम मनुष्य कामनाके कारण फलमें आसक्त होकर बँध जाता है ॥
englishTranslation: The disciplined one gives up the fruit of action and attains steady peace. The undisciplined one, clinging to results through desire, becomes bound.
essence: Peace comes when action is no longer chained to reward.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Letting go of results brings steady peace. Wanting a specific reward keeps you trapped.
simpleInsight: There is relief in doing your part without carrying the weight of the result.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna stands frozen between duty and fear. Krishna explains that action itself does not bind; craving the reward does. The one who gives up attachment to results finds steady peace.
modernRelevance: You send the message, then keep checking for the reply. The knot in your chest is not from the action — it is from needing a certain outcome.
translationLiteral: युक्तः (disciplined one) / कर्मफलम् (fruit of action) / त्यक्त्वा (having given up) / शान्तिम् (peace) / आप्नोति (attains) / नैष्ठिकीम् (steady, abiding) । / अयुक्तः (undisciplined one) / कामकारेण (through desire) / फले (in the fruit) / सक्तः (attached) / निबध्यते (is bound)
